title: Tunneling spectra of $\mathrm{TaO}_x$ junctions for van der Waals superconductors
authors: Yixuan Niu, Jun Cheng, Shiji Ding, Zhongxin Guo, Shang Wang, Chenglong Li, Meining Zhang, Peng Cai
abstract: Tunneling spectroscopy and its evolution are crucial for elucidating the intricate electronic structure and emergent phenomena in quantum materials.Nevertheless, high-quality measurements -- specifically those tracking evolution across temperature and external fields -- remain a formidable challenge. We have fabricated a high-quality $\mathrm{TaO}_x$-based planar tunneling junction by using magnetron sputtering for van der Waals (vdW) superconductors. Using the vdW superconductor $\mathrm{Bi}_2\mathrm{Sr}_2\mathrm{CaCu}_2\mathrm{O}_{8+δ}$ (Bi2212) as a benchmark, this platform yields high-quality tunneling spectra, reproducing the electronic signatures obtained from scanning tunneling spectra acquired from atomically clean surfaces under ultra-high vacuum conditions. This architecture enables high-precision spectroscopy across extensive temperature and magnetic field ranges, offering a universal strategy for probing the electronic structures of diverse two-dimensional systems and facilitating future explorations of material properties.
summary: This paper presents a new method for studying van der Waals superconductors using a stable, TaOx-based planar tunneling junction. The platform allows for high-precision spectroscopy across large temperature and magnetic field ranges, successfully replicating complex electronic features like the pseudogap seen in Bi2212. This provides a robust alternative to Scanning Tunneling Spectroscopy for probing the properties of 2D quantum materials.
main_problem: The difficulty of performing high-quality, stable tunneling spectroscopy to track electronic evolution across temperature and magnetic fields in van der Waals superconductors, due to the limitations of Scanning Tunneling Spectroscopy (STS) and the technical hurdles of traditional planar junction barriers.
main_result: The development of a high-quality TaOx-based planar tunneling junction that successfully reproduces the intrinsic electronic signatures of Bi2212, such as the superconducting gap and pseudogap, with superior stability across wide temperature and magnetic field ranges.
method: Magnetron sputtering was used to fabricate a TaOx-based barrier heterostructure, and planar tunneling spectroscopy was performed alongside transport measurements to analyze differential conductance and resistance.
model_or_system: A planar tunneling junction architecture consisting of a multilayer stack (substrate, Au, Ta, TaOx) in contact with a van der Waals superconductor flake, specifically Bi2Sr2CaCu2O8+d (Bi2212).
key_observables: Differential conductance (dI/dV), superconducting energy gap (Delta_SC), transition temperature (Tc), pseudogap, and resistance-temperature (R-T) curves.
important_parameters: Temperature range (1.5 K to 250 K), magnetic field up to 8 T, and bias voltage.
main_assumptions: The TaOx barrier is assumed to be sufficiently uniform and stable to act as a reliable proxy for ultra-high vacuum STS measurements.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the device fabrication workflow and architecture; Fig 2 displays temperature-dependent transport and spectroscopy data including Tc identification; Fig 3 shows the evolution of dI/dV spectra under varying temperature and magnetic fields; Fig 4 provides a comparative analysis of the TaOx junction against STS and AlOx junctions.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the technical challenge of spectroscopy in vdW materials, describes the fabrication of the TaOx-based junction, presents experimental validation using Bi2212 (including temperature and field dependence), and concludes with a comparison to established spectroscopic methods.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
